notes:
Pickleheads lists 6 Alameda locations totaling 36 courts (10 indoor across 2 locations, 26 outdoor across 5 locations): Lincoln Park (4), Leydecker Park (4), The Hub Sports Club - Alameda (8 indoor + 7 outdoor = 15), Harbor Bay Club (7), Alameda Point Gym (2 indoor), and South Shore Apartments (4) — that math reconciles exactly (4+4+15+7+2+4=36; 8+2=10 indoor; 4+4+7+7+4=26 outdoor across 5 outdoor-having locations), confirming the full 6-venue list. All 6 are included below, but two are flagged confirmed:false with detailed caveats rather than silently included as solid: Alameda Point Gym (the city's own facility page for this exact building tags it only 'Gymnasium' with no pickleball feature, and 30 Google reviews never mention pickleball) and South Shore Apartments (a private apartment complex whose rooftop court has no confirmed public/non-resident access — Google Maps categorizes the whole property as an 'Apartment rental agency' and none of its 155 reviews mention pickleball). Both are real, both are documented by Pickleheads plus at least one other independent aggregator, but a human should decide whether either belongs in a strictly-public court directory before publishing. Primary source used throughout: the City of Alameda's own Recreation & Parks pages (alamedaca.gov), cross-checked venue-by-venue against Pickleheads, and secondarily against DailyPB, Bounce, PicklePlay-style aggregators (Pickleballify, findmypickleballcourt.com, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, places2play.org, globalpickleball.network), Google Maps, Yelp, and for The Hub specifically, a local news writeup (Alameda Post) of its May 2024 grand opening. 

Three other candidate venues were actively investigated and EXCLUDED rather than padded in: (1) Longfellow Park — Bounce.game lists a '1 free court' here, but the official alamedaca.gov Longfellow Park page tags only 'Tennis Courts' with no pickleball feature, Pickleheads' 6-location list doesn't include it either, and Bounce's own address for it (1531 Linden St) looks like a data error confusing Longfellow's cross-street (Linden Street) with a house number — the official page gives the real address as 520 Lincoln Avenue. Too weakly sourced and directly contradicted by the primary source to include. (2) Washington Park — the city's own Sports Facilities page states 'Pickleball Court Rentals are not available at this time,' and one secondary source went further, reporting the park has 'started becoming more strict on not allowing pickleball' (i.e. informal play on the tennis courts is now being actively discouraged/prohibited), despite earlier (unrealized) city discussion about converting a court. Excluded. (3) Estuary Park — Phase 2 construction will add fenced pickleball courts per the city's own project page, but it's a bid/construction project estimated to finish end of 2026, i.e. not yet open as of this research date. Not a currently-operating venue, so excluded; worth re-checking later in 2026. Franklin Park was also checked (tennis courts, pool, fields) with no pickleball evidence found anywhere. 

One address conflict worth a human's attention: Leydecker Park. The official alamedaca.gov page states the address twice as '3221 Mecartney Road,' but Google Maps' own pin for 'Leydecker Park' and every third-party aggregator checked (Bounce, etc.) say '3225 Mecartney Rd.' Address field below uses the official city figure per this project's primary-source rule; lat/lon use Google's pin location (the map dot itself doesn't depend on which house number is correct). 

All lat/lon and googleMapsUrl cid values below were extracted directly from Google Maps place URLs (the hex value in the '!1s0x...:0x<hex>' URL segment converted to decimal), then spot-verified by reloading maps.google.com/?cid=<decimal> and confirming it resolves to the correct venue name — done for every venue here, not just spot-checked, given how easy this id is to mismatch by hand.

venues.name: Lincoln Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court; resurfaced specifically for pickleball in Spring 2017, dedicated permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Not specified as fixed court hours; general park hours apply. Courts are gated/locked with a keypad code (posted by ARPD; contact Recreation Supervisor Joey Carroll, 510-747-7555, jcarroll@alamedaca.gov, for access help) rather than run as an open-access facility. Alameda Pickleball Club organizes sessions here Mon/Wed 12-2pm (beginner/recreational), Wed/Sat 9:30am-12:30pm (all levels), and Sat 3-5pm (recreational) per places2play.org — those are club session times, not the only times the courts are accessible.
venues.weather: Alameda is a low-lying bay island — mild, marine-influenced weather year-round (roughly 44-74°F typical, rarely above 85° or below 37°), with morning fog common (especially in summer), breezy afternoons (windiest in May, calmest Oct/Nov), and dry summers with most rain falling Nov-Mar; outdoor courts are rarely too hot or too cold to play.
venues.notes: Highest-confidence venue in this file. Official ARPD page explicitly tags 'Pickle Ball Court' as a park feature and documents the 2017 resurfacing and a 2019 gate upgrade (access is by keypad code, not stated here since it's a physical-access detail rather than a directory fact — contact ARPD). Address, 4-court count, free/walk-up, and dedicated-lines-and-nets status all agree across alamedaca.gov, Pickleheads, places2play.org, Patch, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, and Google Maps reviews (30 reviews explicitly tag 'pickleball'). Google Maps also shows a separate sub-listing 'Pickleball Courts | Lincoln Park' (5.0, 1 review) nested inside the park; googleMapsUrl here points at the main Lincoln Park listing (4.6, 646 reviews), matching how this repo links other multi-amenity parks.

venues.name: Leydecker Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard tennis courts shared with pickleball via portable nets (not dedicated pickleball-only courts)
venues.netType: Portable nets on shared tennis courts
venues.hours: Not specified beyond general park hours (dawn-dusk per ARPD park rules)
venues.weather: Alameda is a low-lying bay island — mild, marine-influenced weather year-round (roughly 44-74°F typical, rarely above 85° or below 37°), with morning fog common (especially in summer), breezy afternoons (windiest in May, calmest Oct/Nov), and dry summers with most rain falling Nov-Mar; outdoor courts are rarely too hot or too cold to play.
venues.notes: Existence and 4-court/free/outdoor facts are solidly corroborated (official ARPD page publishes a dedicated 'Pickleball Net Code' access document for these courts, confirming pickleball is officially supported here even though 'Pickle Ball Court' isn't one of the page's feature tags — only 'Tennis Courts' is tagged, consistent with this being a shared tennis/pickleball setup via portable nets rather than dedicated pickleball courts). ADDRESS CONFLICT: the official city page states '3221 Mecartney Road' twice; Google Maps' own pin and Bounce.game both say '3225 Mecartney Rd.' Used the official figure per this project's rule that the city recreation department listing is primary; a human should double check the street number against a physical sign or the linked park diagram PDF. lat/lon are Google's pin for 'Leydecker Park' regardless of which house number is correct.

venues.name: The Hub Sports Club - Alameda
venues.surface: Indoor: PPA-approved Acrytech cushioned surface (8 courts). Outdoor: hard court (7 courts). All dedicated, permanent lines and nets.
venues.price: Membership required — Gold Fitness + Pickleball membership $135/month plus a $69 one-time initiation fee, or a $35 drop-in ('Members for the Day') fee for non-members when space permits
venues.weather: Alameda is a low-lying bay island — mild, marine-influenced weather year-round (roughly 44-74°F typical, rarely above 85° or below 37°), with morning fog common (especially in summer), breezy afternoons (windiest in May, calmest Oct/Nov), and dry summers with most rain falling Nov-Mar; outdoor courts are rarely too hot or too cold to play.
venues.notes: Very well corroborated: Pickleheads, the club's own site (thehubpickleball.com), a local news feature (Alameda Post, covering its May 2024 grand opening weekend), Yelp (26 reviews), findmypickleballcourt.com, and DailyPB all agree on 8 indoor + 7 outdoor = 15 courts at this address. Alameda Post reports it's the home venue of Major League Pickleball's Bay Area Breakers and part of a multi-city 'The HUB Pickleball' chain (also has locations in Silicon Valley, San Diego, Jacksonville, etc.) — so 'Alameda' in the name is a location disambiguator, not a local-only operator. lights:true is sourced specifically to Alameda Post's 'state-of-the-art LED lighting' description, not assumed.

venues.name: Harbor Bay Club
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated permanent lines and nets, lighted
venues.price: Membership required (Individual/Couple/Family/Senior tiers; exact monthly pricing not published online — club asks that you call or fill out a membership inquiry form)
venues.skill: Lessons and clinics offered for all skill levels with certified coaches
venues.weather: Alameda is a low-lying bay island — mild, marine-influenced weather year-round (roughly 44-74°F typical, rarely above 85° or below 37°), with morning fog common (especially in summer), breezy afternoons (windiest in May, calmest Oct/Nov), and dry summers with most rain falling Nov-Mar; outdoor courts are rarely too hot or too cold to play.
venues.notes: Solidly corroborated across Pickleheads, the club's own site, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, Pickleballify, and Yelp (145 reviews, 3 explicitly tagged 'pickleball'). Court count (7), lighted/outdoor/dedicated status, membership requirement, and online-reservation model (book up to 8 days ahead) all agree. Membership price itself is deliberately not published online by the club (confirmed by searching multiple pages) — described that gap directly rather than guessing a number.

venues.name: Alameda Point Gym
venues.surface: Indoor multi-purpose gymnasium floor (shared with basketball/volleyball courts); portable pickleball setup implied, not confirmed
venues.hours: Not specified for pickleball specifically; general open-gym hours apply (contact ARPD)
venues.notes: REAL CONFLICT, left unresolved on purpose. Pickleheads and Bounce.game both list this as a 2-indoor-court pickleball venue (address and phone/email agree across both). But: (1) the City of Alameda's own facility page for this exact building — same address, same 'Joey Carroll' contact used for Lincoln/Leydecker's pickleball info — tags it only 'Gymnasium' with features 'Basketball Courts, Gymnasium, Restrooms, Volleyball Courts' and no pickleball mention at all; (2) the city's own Sports Facilities page groups Alameda Point Gym under 'Fields, Outdoor Basketball Courts, and Alameda Point Gym' separately from its 'Tennis Courts (Pickleball Court Rentals are not available at this time)' line; (3) Google Maps' 30 reviews for this address describe basketball, Jiu Jitsu, and wrestling repeatedly and never once mention pickleball; (4) Bounce.game's own page for this venue shows 'No upcoming programs... quiet right now' and 0 reviews. Best guess: pickleball here (if it happens at all) is an occasional portable-net setup on the basketball/volleyball floor, organized informally (e.g. via Bounce meetups) rather than a standing city program or fixed courts — but that's inference, not something a source states outright. Left confirmed:false rather than guessing; a human who can call ARPD (510-747-7555) or visit in person should verify before this goes live.

venues.name: South Shore Apartments
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court on a rooftop sports park, permanent lines, portable nets
venues.price: Not specified as a standalone public fee — this is a private amenity of South Shore Apartments; no source confirms public or non-resident drop-in access
venues.skill: Beginner, intermediate, and advanced pickleball leagues organized via MyOPL.net
venues.weather: Alameda is a low-lying bay island — mild, marine-influenced weather year-round (roughly 44-74°F typical, rarely above 85° or below 37°), with morning fog common (especially in summer), breezy afternoons (windiest in May, calmest Oct/Nov), and dry summers with most rain falling Nov-Mar; outdoor courts are rarely too hot or too cold to play.
venues.notes: REAL SCOPE QUESTION, left unresolved on purpose. This is the 6th of Pickleheads' 6 Alameda locations (needed to reconcile Pickleheads' stated 36-court/26-outdoor/5-outdoor-location totals — without it the math only reaches 32 courts). It's a genuine, physically real amenity: a 1-acre rooftop 'Sports Park' at a Greystar-managed apartment complex, with 4 pickleball courts (permanent lines, portable nets) plus tennis/basketball/volleyball/shuffleboard, and 3 active skill-tiered leagues run through MyOPL.net. But Google Maps categorizes the whole property as an 'Apartment rental agency,' and none of its 155 Google reviews (which do mention the gym, pools, and beach access) ever mention pickleball, and no source found states whether the MyOPL leagues or the courts themselves are open to anyone who isn't a resident or a resident's invited guest. This site's ethos is a directory of PUBLIC courts; unlike Harbor Bay Club and The Hub (commercial clubs anyone can join by paying), South Shore's court access is tied to signing an apartment lease, which is a different kind of restriction. Included here (rather than dropped silently) because it's real and Pickleheads-listed, matching this repo's existing precedent of including HOA/residency-gated venues (see Rossmoor in Walnut Creek) with a clear caveat — but flagged confirmed:false specifically because public accessibility, the fact that matters most for whether this belongs on the site at all, could not be confirmed either way. A human should decide whether to publish this one.
